themeWords.meaning: Zion; the hill in Jerusalem, often representing the city and God’s people
to bear, beget, bring forth; to be born

sentenceTheme: A hymn exalting Zion as God’s chosen city, where nations are registered as born and which the LORD establishes.

verses.text: His foundation is in the holy mountains.

highlightedWords.meaning: holiness; something sacred or set apart

verses.text: The LORD loveth the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

highlightedWords.meaning: the personal, covenant name of the God of Israel
Zion; the hill in Jerusalem, often representing the city and God’s people

verses.text: Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city of God. Selah.

highlightedWords.meaning: a liturgical or musical pause; a marker to pause and reflect

verses.text: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon to them that know me: behold Philistia, and Tyre, with Ethiopia; this man was born there.

highlightedWords.meaning: Cush; Ethiopia, a region south of Egypt

verses.text: And of Zion it shall be said, This and that man was born in her: and the highest himself shall establish her.

highlightedWords.meaning: Zion; the hill in Jerusalem, often representing the city and God’s people
to establish, set up, make firm

verses.text: The LORD shall count, when he writeth up the people, that this man was born there. Selah.
As well the singers as the players on instruments shall be there: all my springs are in thee.
